---
layout: paper
title: "Neural Access Method™ v1.0 — Visualizations"
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX"
---

# Neural Access Method™ v1.0 — Visualizations

**Back to:** [Main Paper](index.html) | [Cross-References](nam-cross-references.html) | [Bibliography](nam-master-bibliography.html)

---

## Figure 1: The Four-Step Protocol

ACCESS → REFRAME → SIMPLIFY → IGNITE

| Step | What It Does | When to Use |
|---|---|---|
| **ACCESS** | Find the pre-built wire — scan for existing pathways the system already owns | Always first — establish the route before delivery |
| **REFRAME** | Shift from internal/anatomical to external/functional focus | When the analytical door is blocked |
| **SIMPLIFY** | Distill to a one- or two-word trigger — cognitive load to zero | When time is compressed or load is high |
| **IGNITE** | Anchor successful execution to identity, not outcome | After correct execution — make it sticky |

---

## Figure 2: Position in Neural Formation Architecture

```
Neural Formation Architecture  ← public container
        ↓
Neuroformation™                ← five-layer architecture (what is failing)
        ↓
Elevation Grid™ 3×3            ← diagnostic coordinate (where it is failing)
        ↓
Neural Access Method™          ← transmission protocol (how to reach it)
        ↓
Adapted Architecture           ← output state (what forms when rebuilt)
```

**The rule:** Never deliver NAM without an Elevation Grid™ coordinate. The Grid tells you where to aim. NAM delivers the correction.

---

## Figure 3: Protocol × Elevation Grid™ Mapping

| Elevation Grid™ Position | Primary NAM Entry | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Row 1 (Hardware/Autonomic) | ACCESS → SIMPLIFY | Physiological pathway — fast, direct |
| Row 2 (Software/Cognitive) | REFRAME → SIMPLIFY | Pattern recognition pathway |
| Row 3 (Architecture/Identity) | ACCESS → IGNITE | Identity anchor required |

---

## Figure 4: What Blocks the Analytical Door

Under load, the analytical pathway closes. NAM routes around it.

| Blocked Pathway | What Closes It | NAM Route |
|---|---|---|
| Verbal instruction | Amygdala response (LeDoux, 1996) | Pre-myelinated analogy |
| Technical correction | Prefrontal bandwidth depletion (Arnsten, 2009) | One-word trigger |
| Motivation/mindset | Identity fracture at Layer 4 | IGNITE to identity anchor |

---

*2026 © Aaron M. Slusher · Independent Researcher · [Main Paper](index.html)*
